summary: Excellent Betty Cantor soundboard captures a high‑energy 1978 show highlighted by a monster Franklin’s Tower, rippin’ Cassidy and sweet Peggy‑O.
review: The recording is a rich, flawless Betty board that lets the 1978‑style frenzy shine through – power‑chord guitars, wild vocal harmonies and nonstop banter. Standout moments include an insane Franklin’s Tower, ripping Cassidy, sweet Peggy‑O and a soaring Passenger, all delivered with the band “going nuts” in true spring ’78 fashion.

ai_rating.rationale: All seven reviews are overwhelmingly positive (average 4.7★), praising both the musical performance and the pristine soundboard recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound rich, perfect and great; no technical complaints were noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with high energy, loose banter and classic ’78 improvisational chaos; jokes and vocal interplay add to the fun atmosphere.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia goes off on guitar, swaps lyrics mid‑song and delivers the wild power‑chord driven playing that defines the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocals are front‑and‑center – high notes in Passenger, a ‘left‑hand monkey wrench’ comment about his thank‑you interjections, plus several jokes between tunes.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux is noted for “making his own way” on keys; Brent (mentioned humorously) also gets praise.
